title: Grilled Crumbly Korean Sweet Potato Crumble
description: A rich and crumbly dish perfect for a date-night Ukrainian meal, featuring sweet potatoes with kiwifruit in a savory sauce that is also fodmap-friendly.

ingredients.name: sweet potatoes
ingredients.notes: preferably baby sweet potatoes for a more tender texture

ingredients.name: kiwi fruit
ingredients.notes: preferably smaller varieties such as Hayward or Snowpatch kiwifruit, to maintain moisture and prevent squishing

ingredients.name: maple syrup
ingredients.notes: can be adjusted based on sweetness preference

ingredients.name: pomegranate molasses
ingredients.notes: optional, adds a touch of complexity to the dish

ingredients.name: cinnamon
ingredients.notes: gives sweetness and depth of flavor

ingredients.name: flaky salt
ingredients.amount: for sprinkling on top of the crumble, optional

instructions: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat.
Wash and dry sweet potatoes thoroughly. Cut them into small pieces and place in a pot with enough cold water to cover them.
Bring the water to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to maintain a simmer for about 10 minutes or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Drain the sweet potatoes well in a colander. Let it cool slightly before mashing them gently using a fork.
In a mixing bowl, combine the mashed sweet potatoes with kiwi fruit chunks, maple syrup, unsalted butter, pomegranate molasses (if used), and cinnamon.
Season to taste with salt. Stir well until all pieces of sweet potato are coated evenly.
Transfer the mixture onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a silicone mat for easy removal.
Sprinkle flaky salt on top if desired, then place into preheated grill, cooking for 8-10 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Serve hot as a sweet potato crumble dish. Enjoy the rich and crumbly texture paired with kiwi fruit's freshness.
